mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, mechanically increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5%, it signals a labor market cooling that historically precedes Federal Reserve policy pivots, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain negative or flat in >50% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit expansion (RSAFS) combined with rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates synchronized demand-pull inflation and robust corporate earnings growth, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or zero predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when both series rise simultaneously over a 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) in a low-interest-rate environment stimulate wealth effects and construction-related earnings, boosting S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to generate positive 60-day forward returns in >60% of observations when FEDFUNDS remains below 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A CPI reading exceeding 3.5% triggers repricing of inflation expectations, forcing higher discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL annualized growth surpasses 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth (PAYEMS) coupled with contracting consumer credit (RSAFS) signals an unsustainable consumption boom driven by leverage, which typically culminates in a sharp equity drawdown as credit constraints bite.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive Rank IC (>0.05) in forward 20-day returns during credit contraction phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding capacity utilization and healthy corporate throughput, directly supporting earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between INDPRO month-over-month changes and subsequent 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 1.5% annualized rate signals a demand shock that erodes corporate profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5%.
